sections.title: What doesn't apply (and why)
sections.content:
- **N+1 queries**: no database, no network layer, no repository/ORM anywhere in the codebase. N/A.
- **Memory leaks**: no `StatefulWidget`, no `AnimationController`/`StreamController`/listeners, nothing with a `dispose()` obligation exists yet â€” everything is `StatelessWidget` or pure functions. Nothing to leak. Watch this in the next wave: `saga_scroll_physics.dart`'s inertia math is wired to nothing yet, but when it gets hooked to a ticker/`AnimationController`, that's where a missing `dispose()` would first become possible.

sections.title: Real finding: painter repaints every frame regardless of content (the "unnecessary re-render" equivalent)
Redundant computation â€” real but not worth fixing yet
sections.content: `nodeAt`/`x(index)` (`saga_path.dart`) recompute `sin()` for every node in the ~27-item window on every single frame, even though geometry only depends on `index` (not `progress`), and only the projector's output actually needs to change per-frame. Technically redundant â€” but 27 `sin()` calls/frame is nanoseconds, not a bottleneck on any target device. Caching this now would be complexity for a win that doesn't exist. Skip; revisit only if profiling ever shows `nodeAt` on a hot path (it won't).

sections.title: Caching opportunities
sections.content:
None to add today â€” the whole pipeline is stateless/pure by design (that's explicitly documented in the file comments), and the one place identity-based memoization matters (`shouldRepaint`) is the fix above, not a cache.

Execution Ledger
- Route: main-direct (read all 13 lib files directly; too small to warrant repo-scanner)
- Model/process: main session, Sonnet 5
- Tools: 3 Bash + 12 Read calls
- Token class: small
- Result: Most of the requested checklist (N+1, memory leaks, React re-renders) doesn't map onto this pre-wiring Flutter/Flame codebase; one real bug found (SagaScene lacks value equality â†’ shouldRepaint always fires) with a concrete fix.
- Next: ask whether to apply the equality-fix diffs now, or hold until the screen is actually wired to CustomPaint.

codeBlocks.code:
bool shouldRepaint(covariant SagaMapPainter oldDelegate) =>
    oldDelegate.scene != scene;

rawOutputPreview:
Read the whole `lib/` tree (13 Dart files, ~360 lines) â€” this is a Flutter/CustomPainter game screen (Flame-adjacent), not a React/SQL app, and it's pre-wiring: `SagaMapScreen` is still an empty `Scaffold()`, nothing renders yet. So most of the requested checklist maps to "doesn't exist here" rather than "found and ignored." Reporting honestly rather than padding with invented findings.

## What doesn't apply (and why)

- **N+1 queries**: no database, no network layer, no repository/ORM anywhere in the codebase. N/A.
- **Memory leaks**: no `StatefulWidget`, no `AnimationController`/`StreamController`/listeners, nothing with a `dispose()` obligation exists yet â€” everything is `StatelessWidget` or pure functions. Nothing to leak. Watch this in the next wave: `saga_scroll_physics.dart`'s inertia math is wired to nothing yet, but when it gets hooked to a ticker/`AnimationController`, that's where a missing `dispose()` would first become possible.

## Real finding: painter repaints every frame regardless of content (the "unnecessary re-render" equivalent)

`lib/saga_map/rendering/saga_map_painter.dart:77-78`:
```dart
bool shouldRepaint(covariant SagaMapPainter oldDelegate) =>
    oldDelegate.scene != scene;
```
`SagaScene` (`saga_scene.dart`) has no `==`/`hashCode` override, and `buildSagaScene` (`saga_scene_builder.dart:13`) constructs a **brand-new** `SagaScene` every call. So `!=` is identity comparison on a value that's never the same object twice â€” `shouldRepaint` always returns `true`, even when `progress`/`currentLevel` haven't changed and the frame is visually identical. This is exactly the React "new object reference every render defeats `React.memo`" bug, just in `CustomPainter` form. It'll cause every rebuild to trigger a full repaint of every visible stone once the screen is actually wired up.

Fix: give the scene graph value equality, reusing the pattern already in `SagaMapState` (rung 2 â€” copy what's already in the codebase, don't invent a new convention):
